name: Half Adder
description: A Half Adder is a digital circuit that performs addition of two single-bit binary numbers. It has two inputs (A and B) and two outputs: Sum (XOR of inputs) and Carry (AND of inputs). Truth table: 0+0=00, 0+1=01, 1+0=01, 1+1=10.

steps.instruction: Welcome! A Half Adder adds two single-bit binary numbers. It produces two outputs: Sum and Carry.
steps.expected_action: Observe the two input terminals labeled A and B.

steps.instruction: The XOR gate produces the Sum output. XOR outputs 1 when inputs differ, 0 when they match.
steps.expected_action: Toggle inputs A and B to see the XOR behavior.

steps.instruction: The AND gate produces the Carry output. Carry is 1 only when both inputs are 1.
steps.expected_action: Set both A=1 and B=1 to see Carry=1.
Verify the truth table by testing all input combinations.

steps.instruction: Congratulations! You've explored the Half Adder. Notice how the Carry bit represents overflow when adding 1+1=10 in binary.
steps.expected_action: Great job! The Half Adder is the foundation for more complex arithmetic circuits.
